weaning.definition: Gradually changing a baby from drinking only milk to eating solid food.
weaning.example: She was weaning her daughter from breast milk to solid food.

vegetables.definition: Plants that are eaten as food, like carrots and broccoli.
vegetables.example: The child ate her vegetables while being sung to.

spaghetti.example: The child ate spaghetti sitting on her rug.

rug.definition: A soft piece of thick cloth that covers part of a floor.
rug.example: The child sat on her special rug in the middle of the room.

fantasize.definition: To imagine something you wish would happen, but probably will not.
fantasize.example: Parents sometimes fantasize about future problems that never happen.

pacifier.definition: A rubber object that a baby sucks on to feel calm.
pacifier.example: She worried her child would never give up her pacifier.

accompany.definition: To go somewhere together with someone.
accompany.example: Will Mr Squidgy have to accompany him to work?

phase.definition: A temporary period or stage that someone goes through.
phase.example: Almost everything with children is a phase.

odd.example: Go with what works, however odd it seems.

implement.definition: To start using a new plan or system.
implement.example: If what works stops working, implement a change.

modelling.definition: Showing someone how to behave by doing it yourself.
modelling.example: Modelling flexibility makes change easier for everyone.

flexibility.definition: The ability to change or adapt easily.
flexibility.example: Flexibility is an important skill for both parents and children.

snoring.definition: Making loud breathing sounds while sleeping.
snoring.example: Eventually your children will get fed up with your snoring.

pile.definition: To put a lot of things on top of each other.
pile.example: They piled the whole family into two double beds pushed together.

lunchtimes.definition: The times during the day when people eat lunch.
lunchtimes.example: She worried about her daughter's behaviour at school lunchtimes.

refuse.definition: To say no to something; to not accept.
refuse.example: What if she always refuses to eat at a table?

date.definition: A planned meeting with someone you are interested in romantically.
date.example: She even worried about her daughter's first date.

restaurants.definition: Places where you pay to eat meals prepared by others.
restaurants.example: She worried her child would not behave in restaurants.
